evidence: Implementation-level closure of FGAP-136 (write-schema replace version-bump had no tool-surface migration path) per plan ~/.claude/plans/idempotent-dancing-wilkes.md across 5 commits on branch fgap-136-schema-migration-tool: 22d7d24 (step 1 — migrations.schema.json + migrations-store + migrationsPath path-builder), 18ce8d0 (step 2 — migration-registry-loader with identity + 4 declarative-transform ops + per-cwd caching with invalidation hook), 77b0f9f (step 3 — write-schema-migration Pi tool registered with writer.kind=human body-level enforcement + operation dispatch), dd85196 (step 4 — getProjectMigrationRegistry wired into writeBlock + readBlock validation paths; end-to-end version-bump + identity migration unblocked), bbad988 (step 5 — auth-gate routing + write-schema description abstract-language rewrite + SKILL regen). With this arc landed an in-pi operator authoring at writer.kind=human (gated through pi-dispatch auth-gate) can declare a schema version-bump migration via the write-schema-migration Pi tool; the declaration persists to <contextDir>/migrations.json under the new bundled migrations.schema.json; subsequent writeBlock/readBlock against the version-bumped schema walk the data forward through the registered MigrationFn without process restart (cache invalidation hook fires after each mutation).

criteria_results.criterion: build / check / test green across the workspace
criteria_results.evidence: npm run build exit 0 across all 5 packages; npm run check exit 0 (biome 252 files + tsc --noEmit clean); npm test exit 0 with per-package totals pi-agent-dispatch 62/62, pi-context 725/725 (was 670 pre-arc; +55 = 15 migrations-store + 22 migration-registry-loader + 16 write-schema-migration-tool + 2 schema-migration-integration), pi-workflows 158/157 (1 pre-existing skip), pi-behavior-monitors 830/828 (2 pre-existing skips). 0 failures introduced.

criteria_results.criterion: write-time integration: bump schema + identity migration unblocks block write that would have thrown version-mismatch
criteria_results.evidence: packages/pi-context/src/schema-migration-integration.test.ts scenario 1: write block at v1 → bump schema v2 → writeBlock with v1 schema_version against v2 schema throws (asserts file is byte-unchanged) → writeSchemaMigrationExecute kind=identity v1→v2 → subsequent writeBlock succeeds. Asserts the registry singleton picks up the persisted declaration without process restart (the cache invalidation hook on the mutation helper is what makes this work).

criteria_results.criterion: declarative-transform integration: bumped schema with operator-declared transform produces migrated read shape
criteria_results.evidence: packages/pi-context/src/schema-migration-integration.test.ts scenario 2: write block v1 → bump schema v2 (envelope-shape change) + write-schema-migration kind=declarative-transform with composed set ops (schema_version + label) → readBlock walks v1 on-disk forward to v2 on read, asserting the new field landed in the returned shape. Validates the per-op walker (set creating intermediate parents) end-to-end through the readBlock surface.

criteria_results.criterion: citation-rot scanner regression: 0 hits across 5 packages
criteria_results.evidence: scanForCitationRot({ packageDirs: 5 packages }) returns 0 hits. New prose (auth-gate.ts list comment, auth-gate.test.ts assertion docstring, write-schema description rewrite, write-schema-migration tool description, write-schema-migration-tool.ts head comment) all use abstract framework language without canonical_id substrings on the public-surface paths the scanner walks.
